behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names the file javascript/private/header.bzl, the provider module @rules_closure//closure/private:defs.bzl, the deprecated patterns, and the build targets verified by tests.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction essentially dictates the exact fix: load provider from @rules_closure//closure/private:defs.bzl, use 'ProviderSymbol in target' and 'target[ProviderSymbol].field_name', and update binaries.update() to bracket notation—leaving little reasoning about what to change.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests extract the loaded symbol name dynamically allowing aliases, and only assert structural patterns (in check, bracket access, update call) that any correct migration would share.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests include subprocess calls to 'bazel query', 'bazel build', and 'bazel run buildifier' to actually validate behavior, in addition to text checks.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests require both removal of deprecated patterns AND presence of new patterns AND successful bazel build—stubs cannot satisfy this.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover load statement, removal of deprecated access, new provider check/access, buildifier lint, and bazel build as described in instruction.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are named descriptively (test_load_statement_present, test_no_deprecated_getattr, etc.) with docstrings marking FAIL-TO-PASS vs PASS-TO-PASS.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task does not produce structured data output.

no_typos.reason: Filenames, paths, and target names are consistent between instruction, tests, and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names the exact file (javascript/private/header.bzl) and specifies the three exact lines/patterns to replace, eliminating localization effort.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Gold patch is a small localized diff adding a load statement and replacing two lines in header.bzl.
